items.title: Spirit of Tasmania operator to get $506m government bailout amid bungled ferry rollout

children.content: The announcement comes as TT-Line continues to wrestle with the financial fallout from its bungled delivery of the new Spirit of Tasmania vessels.

gallery.altText: A large white and red ship docked in Hobart.

thumbnail.altText: A large white and red ship docked in Hobart.

items.title: Married couple in 60s in murder-suicide in Tasmania

children.content: Tasmanian police say the deaths of a married couple in their sixties at a home in Campania, north of Hobart, are being treated as a murder-suicide.

image.altText: Police block a rural road with several vehicles.

items.title: Liberty Bell Bay smelter workers get another two weeks pay reprieve

children.content: Workers at Australia's only manganese smelter will be thrown an additional lifeline as the state and federal governments step in with a two-week pay offer. It comes days before a previous short-term deal was due to expire.

image.altText: men and women in orange high vis outside a blue logo and a rusted sculpture

items.title: Chloroform in drain suspected as reason hospital ward shut, staff ill

children.content: An incident that prompted a Tasmanian hospital theatre to evacuate, and left several staff feeling ill, was possibly caused by chloroform, documents reveal.

gallery.altText: A sign with LGH and emergency written on it.

thumbnail.altText: A sign with LGH and emergency written on it.

items.title: Attempted murder charges laid after man shot in stomach

children.content: The man allegedly responsible for a shooting in Tasmania's north west on Wednesday has been charged with attempted murder.

image.altText: Two police officers walk along a residential road.

items.title: Clean-up deadline rejected for contaminated abandoned base

children.content: The Australian government has officially ruled out cleaning up Antarctica's contaminated Wilkes Station before the end of the decade, as previously recommended by a federal parliamentary committee.

image.altText: Images of an abandoned station in the icy and snowy Antarctic wilderness.

items.title: After long absence, Tasmania's short-stay figures just got released

children.content: Almost 500 dwellings in Tasmania have been converted to short-stay accommodation in just over a year. It's the first time short-stay data has been updated since the end of 2024.

image.altText: Woman opens a door with a key

items.title: TasInsure unravelling a lesson for Liberals in promise management

children.content: In a week where "broken promise" is being shouted by the Tasmanian Liberals in support of their federal colleagues' attack on the Labor budget, closer to home, a promise of their own is giving them grief.

image.altText: Jeremy Rockliff smiles.

items.title: TassieCast: Is Tasmania facing a cut to arts?

children.content: The state government has already chopped a number of creative courses from TasTAFE, leading to fears in the arts industry of a state-wide creative brain drain. And as the state budget looms, there are more worries. Airlie Ward chats with state political reporter Josh Duggan.

items.title: Why millennials and gen Z feel ripped off by this budget

children.content: The prime minister says this budget is about fixing intergenerational inequality, but online, gen Z and the millennials are hearing a different narrative.

articles.title: The budget reply is a reminder times have changed and attention is short

children.content: To appreciate how much times have changed, it is worth comparing the opposition leader's budget reply speech to Peter Dutton's.

image.altText: Peter Dutton and Angus Taylor

avatar.alt: A close up photo of Annabel Crabb, who has long brown hair and is wearing red spectacles, red lipstick and a blue top

articles.title: Angus Taylor promises permanent tax cuts and to reinstate all negative gearing

children.content: The Coalition would repeal all of Labor's negative gearing and capital gains changes and spend $22.5 billion over four years handing back tax "bracket creep" to workers if elected.

image.altText: a male politician giving a speech in a chamber

articles.title: AI to power medicines approvals but humans will still call the shots

children.content: This week’s budget revealed a number of ways the federal government is expected to use artificial intelligence to cut back on red tape. One expert says the benefits are "mostly good".

image.altText: Female hands using a computer mouse

items.title: 'We want the straits open': Trump says China agrees on ending the Iran war — as it happened

children.content: Chinese President Xi Jinping praised the building of a "stable relationship" with the US as the two world leaders met in Beijing. Take a look back at the historic state visit. Take a look back at how the day unfolded.

image.altText: Trump and Xi, both wearing suits, look at something out of frame to the left

items.title: National commissioner says review into Kumanjayi Little Baby won't 'cut it'

children.content: The National Commissioner for Indigenous children has criticised the selection of a retired NSW police commissioner and long-standing NT public servant as the leaders of a review into the territory's child protection system.

items.title: How likely is Delta Goodrem to bring home the Eurovision crown?

children.content: In a dazzling show of light and a powerful, pitch-perfect performance, Delta Goodrem may have just given Australia a real shot at winning Eurovision for the first time.

image.altText: Delta Goodrem singing.

articles.title: Delta Goodrem climbs onto her piano and into Eurovision 2026 grand final

children.content: Delta Goodrem will perform in the Eurovision 2026 grand final for Australia after a rousing performance of the song Eclipse during the semifinal.

image.altText: Delta Goodrem standing on a golden piano with jets of fire behind her.

items.title: Israeli nationalists chant anti-Arab slogans in Jerusalem Day march

children.content: Thousands of Israeli nationalists, including young boys, join crowds chanting death threats towards Palestinians while marching through Jerusalem Old City.

image.altText: A large crowd of people with many of them holding up Israeli flags.

items.title: Man with swastika tattooed on leg arrested in Alice Springs

children.content: A 25-year-old man has been arrested and charged in Alice Springs for displaying a tattoo of a swastika on his leg.

image.altText: A patch of the NT police force emblem on an unidentified officer's uniform

items.title: AFP uncover 14kg of cocaine hidden in fruit pulp at Melbourne Airport

children.content: Police say criminal syndicates are becoming more creative at concealing drugs within airport shipments.

items.title: Soon after John quit the Liberal Party, the strange phone calls started

children.content: When John Ternel quit the Victorian Liberal Party this week the party mistakenly sent an email to members saying he had died. Mr Ternel says the ordeal confirmed to him that the Victorian Liberals are in shambles.

image.altText: A man in a light business shirt and dark jacket looks into camera, with office buildings behind him.

items.title: As the eldest daughter, I always knew I would care for my parents

children.content: As the eldest daughter in a Vietnamese family, it was an unspoken expectation I would be the one to care for my aging parents.

gallery.altText: Cindy Mai and her mum when she was 11  years old

items.title: 'Incredible' colonial-era boat used for trade in Sydney Harbour uncovered

children.content: Maritime archaeologists are working to restore the oldest colonial-era boat ever found in Australia, discovered during the construction of the Barangaroo metro station in 2018.

image.altText: A team of eight in high-vis working to excavate boat remains at a build site, the CBD in the background.

items.title: Melanie Lynskey joins the fight for justice after NZ's Pike River Mine disaster

children.content: Standing side by side with real-life heroes Anna Osborne and Sonya Rockhouse, the Yellow Jackets star is a passionate ally for their cause.

image.altText: A group of miners in work gear, helmets and head lamps walking.

items.title: Surfers conquer 'terrifying' tidal rapid in remote WA

children.content: Pro surfers Anthony Walsh and Dylan Graves have conquered the never-before-surfed Talbot Bay tidal rapid in Western Australia's remote north.

thumbnail.altText: A jetski on a big wave

items.title: ASX closes lower, oil price up as Trump and Xi discuss war in Iran — as it happened

children.content: The ASX200 finished the week lower, and oil headed for a weekly gain as the reopening of the critical Strait of Hormuz waterway and end to the war in the Middle East remain in doubt.

image.altText: The silhouette of an oil pumpjack in the foreground with a wind turbine in the background.

items.title: US vote to halt Iran war narrowly defeated as Israel and Lebanon have 'productive' first day of talks

children.content: Israel and Lebanon have reported a productive first day of talks in Washington as the deadline for the ceasefire between the two countries approaches.

image.altText: Emergency responders gather around a burning vehicle at the scene of an Israeli airstrike

items.title: Matthew Wale becomes Solomon Islands prime minister after secret ballot win

children.content: Matthew Wale is the new prime minister of Solomon Islands after winning a vote in the Pacific island nation's parliament.

image.altText: Matthew

items.title: Hospitalisation of young babies almost halved by new immunisation program

children.content: A new immunisation program for pregnant mothers and newborns has almost halved the number of young babies being hospitalised with a serious respiratory virus, according to a new report.

something
await you.

items.title: Sydney GP charged over alleged sexual assault of patients granted bail

items.title: Eight people arrested at North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment site, police say

children.content: Police say eight people have been arrested at the North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment site this morning.

image.altText: A protester sits by a gate of the North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment.

items.title: Trust tax could hurt young, not just ultra-wealthy, tax advisors warn

children.content: The federal budget includes a new 30 per cent minimum tax on discretionary trusts. Experts say it is not just the ultra-wealthy that will be hit.

items.title: Cattle industry loses court appeal over live export ban compensation

children.content: Australia's northern cattle industry says it is "deeply disappointed" by a Federal Court decision to dismiss its appeal over compensation for the 2011 live export ban.

image.altText: the sun sets over cattle cattle yard with dust rising

items.title: Twenty years since greatest live TV blunder when BBC interviewed wrong guy

children.content: Guy Gomo reflects on his infamous live TV mix-up, which has been immortalised as one of the greatest TV blunders of all time.

items.title: Chinese Australians say Coalition pitch to cut non-citizens' welfare 'sounds like One Nation'

children.content: Chinese Australians have lashed the Coalition's plan to block non-citizens from accessing welfare services, likening the opposition's targeting of migrants to One Nation.

items.title: Carlton interim coach Fraser pulls out of running for full-time role

children.content: Josh Fraser will lead Carlton for the remainder of the season after Michael Voss's exit, but isn't prepared to do the job full-time just yet.

items.title: Overdose at Crown Melbourne every two weeks, data shows

children.content: Paramedics have attended more than 50 suspected overdoses at Crown Melbourne in the past two years, data released exclusively to the ABC shows.

image.altText: One of the main entrances to Crown Casino in Melbourne.
